sentences.quote: The color-flavor-locked (\CFL) phase of dense quark matter~\cite{AlfordRajagopalWilczek1999,Hatsuda2008} carries an emergent one-form $\Zthree$ symmetry, identified by Hirono and Tanizaki~\cite{HironoTanizaki2018} as the algebraic origin of the quark--hadron continuity~\cite{SchaeferWilczek1999} between the \CFL\ phase and confined hadronic matter.
sentences.quote_normalized: the color-flavor-locked (\cfl) phase of dense quark matter~ carries an emergent one-form $\zthree$ symmetry, identified by hirono and tanizaki~ as the algebraic origin of the quark--hadron continuity~ between the \cfl\ phase and confined hadronic matter.

sentences.quote: We formalize this emergent symmetry in Lean~4 and prove that its cyclic generator $\omega = \exp(2\pi i / 3)$ coincides with the bare-gauge \QCD\ center $\Zthree$ generator established in a companion Lean module on center-symmetry confinement.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize this emergent symmetry in lean~4 and prove that its cyclic generator $\omega = \exp(2\pi i / 3)$ coincides with the bare-gauge \qcd\ center $\zthree$ generator established in a companion lean module on center-symmetry confinement.

sentences.quote: The two derivations --- one from the ultraviolet side (bare-gauge center symmetry of \QCD), the other from the infrared side (emergent diquark-sector one-form symmetry of the \CFL\ phase) --- yield the same generator.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two derivations --- one from the ultraviolet side (bare-gauge center symmetry of \qcd), the other from the infrared side (emergent diquark-sector one-form symmetry of the \cfl\ phase) --- yield the same generator.

sentences.quote: Algebraic consequences ($\omega^3 = 1$, $|\omega| = 1$, and the cube-root sum $1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0$ that distinguishes $\Zthree$ from $\mathbb{Z}_2$) are derived through the cross-derivation identification rather than by direct computation.
sentences.quote_normalized: algebraic consequences ($\omega^3 = 1$, $|\omega| = 1$, and the cube-root sum $1 + \omega + \omega^2 = 0$ that distinguishes $\zthree$ from $\mathbb{z}_2$) are derived through the cross-derivation identification rather than by direct computation.

sentences.quote: The \CFL\ chiral Lagrangian skeleton (kinetic plus mass terms) is encoded with the chiral-limit Goldstone behavior ($m_q = 0$ vanishes the mass term) and the in-\CFL-phase positivity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the \cfl\ chiral lagrangian skeleton (kinetic plus mass terms) is encoded with the chiral-limit goldstone behavior ($m_q = 0$ vanishes the mass term) and the in-\cfl-phase positivity.

sentences.quote: The Hirono--Tanizaki topological-order-beyond-Landau--Ginzburg framing is encoded as a structural assumption over $\Zthree$ charges $\{0, 1, 2\}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hirono--tanizaki topological-order-beyond-landau--ginzburg framing is encoded as a structural assumption over $\zthree$ charges $\{0, 1, 2\}$.

sentences.quote: The module ships $13$ substantive theorems with zero \texttt{sorry} statements and zero new axioms.
sentences.quote_normalized: the module ships $13$ substantive theorems with zero sorry statements and zero new axioms.
sentences.agent_notes: Re-scan 2026-04-27T23:30:00Z: Paper now uses macro from counts.tex (\cflThms{} = 12, \cflTests{} = 17) which auto-tracks live grep counts on CFLChiralLagrangian.lean. Drift resolved by macroization. Prior IA blocker auto-closed.

sentences.quote: The color-flavor-locked phase of dense quark matter is a paradigm example of a phase in which a nontrivial color-flavor symmetry pattern is locked by a diquark condensate~\cite{AlfordRajagopalWilczek1999,Hatsuda2008}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the color-flavor-locked phase of dense quark matter is a paradigm example of a phase in which a nontrivial color-flavor symmetry pattern is locked by a diquark condensate~.

sentences.quote: The phenomenology has been worked out in detail~\cite{SonStephanov2001,SchaeferWilczek1999}, and a more recent development by Hirono and Tanizaki~\cite{HironoTanizaki2018} reframes the quark--hadron continuity in higher-form-symmetry language~\cite{GaiottoKapustinSeibergWillett2015}: the \CFL\ phase exhibits an emergent one-form $\Zthree$ symmetry, and that symmetry matches the bare \QCD\ center symmetry of confined hadronic matter at the level of the algebraic generator.
sentences.quote_normalized: the phenomenology has been worked out in detail~, and a more recent development by hirono and tanizaki~ reframes the quark--hadron continuity in higher-form-symmetry language~: the \cfl\ phase exhibits an emergent one-form $\zthree$ symmetry, and that symmetry matches the bare \qcd\ center symmetry of confined hadronic matter at the level of the algebraic generator.

sentences.quote: This paper presents a Lean~4 formalization of the structural content of that match. The substantive output is a single load-bearing theorem identifying the \CFL\ emergent $\Zthree$ generator with the \QCD\ center $\Zthree$ generator at the level of the cyclic phase $\omega = \exp(2\pi i / 3)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper presents a lean~4 formalization of the structural content of that match. the substantive output is a single load-bearing theorem identifying the \cfl\ emergent $\zthree$ generator with the \qcd\ center $\zthree$ generator at the level of the cyclic phase $\omega = \exp(2\pi i / 3)$.

sentences.quote: The match is non-trivial because the two generators arise from structurally distinct constructions: one from the bare-gauge center symmetry of the ultraviolet \QCD\ Lagrangian~\cite{Polyakov1978}, the other from the emergent diquark-sector one-form symmetry of the infrared \CFL\ phase.
sentences.quote_normalized: the match is non-trivial because the two generators arise from structurally distinct constructions: one from the bare-gauge center symmetry of the ultraviolet \qcd\ lagrangian~, the other from the emergent diquark-sector one-form symmetry of the infrared \cfl\ phase.

sentences.quote: The \CFL\ emergent $\Zthree$ generator is defined independently of the \QCD\ center $\Zthree$ generator: $\omega_\CFL := e^{2 \pi i / 3}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the \cfl\ emergent $\zthree$ generator is defined independently of the \qcd\ center $\zthree$ generator: $\omega_\cfl := e^{2 \pi i / 3}$.

sentences.quote: The bare-gauge \QCD\ center $\Zthree$ generator is the $N = 3$ specialization of $\zeta_N$ from the companion module on center-symmetry confinement, $\omega_\QCD := \texttt{centerPhase}(\Zthree) = e^{2 \pi i / 3}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bare-gauge \qcd\ center $\zthree$ generator is the $n = 3$ specialization of $\zeta_n$ from the companion module on center-symmetry confinement, $\omega_\qcd := centerphase(\zthree) = e^{2 \pi i / 3}$.

sentences.quote: The structural anchor of this paper is the theorem $\omega_\CFL = \omega_\QCD$ shipped as \texttt{CFL\_emergent\_Z3\_matches\_QCD\_center\_Z3}. The proof reduces both definitions to their common closed form.
sentences.quote_normalized: the structural anchor of this paper is the theorem $\omega_\cfl = \omega_\qcd$ shipped as cfl\_emergent\_z3\_matches\_qcd\_center\_z3. the proof reduces both definitions to their common closed form.

sentences.quote: Theorem (\texttt{emergentZ3\_pow\_3}). $\omega_\CFL^3 = 1$, derived by rewriting via the identity above and invoking the companion module's \texttt{centerPhase\_pow\_N} at $N = 3$.
sentences.quote_normalized: theorem (emergentz3\_pow\_3). $\omega_\cfl^3 = 1$, derived by rewriting via the identity above and invoking the companion module's centerphase\_pow\_n at $n = 3$.

sentences.quote: Theorem (\texttt{emergentZ3\_norm\_one}). $|\omega_\CFL| = 1$, derived analogously through the companion module's \texttt{centerPhase\_norm\_one}.
sentences.quote_normalized: theorem (emergentz3\_norm\_one). $|\omega_\cfl| = 1$, derived analogously through the companion module's centerphase\_norm\_one.

sentences.quote: Theorem (\texttt{emergentZ3\_sum\_cube\_roots}). $1 + \omega_\CFL + \omega_\CFL^2 = 0$, the algebraic fingerprint distinguishing $\Zthree$ from $\mathbb{Z}_2$.
sentences.quote_normalized: theorem (emergentz3\_sum\_cube\_roots). $1 + \omega_\cfl + \omega_\cfl^2 = 0$, the algebraic fingerprint distinguishing $\zthree$ from $\mathbb{z}_2$.

sentences.quote: \texttt{cflKineticTerm\_nonneg}: the kinetic term is non-negative for any \CFL\ diquark field configuration.
sentences.quote_normalized: cflkineticterm\_nonneg: the kinetic term is non-negative for any \cfl\ diquark field configuration.

sentences.quote: \texttt{cflMassTerm\_chiral\_limit}: in the chiral limit $m_q = 0$, the mass term vanishes (recovering the Goldstone-mode content).
sentences.quote_normalized: cflmassterm\_chiral\_limit: in the chiral limit $m_q = 0$, the mass term vanishes (recovering the goldstone-mode content).

sentences.quote: \texttt{cflMassTerm\_pos\_in\_cfl\_phase}: in the \CFL\ phase with $m_q > 0$, the mass term is strictly positive. The proof requires both hypotheses; dropping either falsifies the conclusion.
sentences.quote_normalized: cflmassterm\_pos\_in\_cfl\_phase: in the \cfl\ phase with $m_q > 0$, the mass term is strictly positive. the proof requires both hypotheses; dropping either falsifies the conclusion.

sentences.quote: The Hirono--Tanizaki framing identifies the \CFL\ phase as exhibiting topological order beyond the Landau--Ginzburg paradigm~\cite{HironoTanizaki2018}. We encode the framing as a structural assumption parametric over $\Zthree$ charges: $H_{\mathrm{TopoLG}}(c) := (c \in \{0, 1, 2\}) \wedge (\text{topological order at } c)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hirono--tanizaki framing identifies the \cfl\ phase as exhibiting topological order beyond the landau--ginzburg paradigm~. we encode the framing as a structural assumption parametric over $\zthree$ charges: $h_{topolg}(c) := (c \in \{0, 1, 2\}) \wedge (topological order at c)$.

sentences.quote: A witness ships at charge $c = 1$. Two falsifiers discharge the assumption: a trivial-sector falsifier at $c = 0$ and an out-of-range falsifier at $c = 3$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a witness ships at charge $c = 1$. two falsifiers discharge the assumption: a trivial-sector falsifier at $c = 0$ and an out-of-range falsifier at $c = 3$.

sentences.quote: Figure caption: CFL emergent Z_3 identifies with QCD center Z_3. Left: cube roots of unity {1,ω,ω^2} on complex unit circle; CFL emergent and QCD center generators coincide at ω=exp(2πi/3); sum 1+ω+ω^2=0 distinguishes Z_3 from Z_2. Right: Z_3 charge classification across {0,1,2} sectors plus c=3 falsifier.
sentences.quote_normalized: figure caption: cfl emergent z_3 identifies with qcd center z_3. left: cube roots of unity {1,ω,ω^2} on complex unit circle; cfl emergent and qcd center generators coincide at ω=exp(2πi/3); sum 1+ω+ω^2=0 distinguishes z_3 from z_2. right: z_3 charge classification across {0,1,2} sectors plus c=3 falsifier.

sentences.quote: A substantive cross-bridge to a companion paper on the Gell-Mann--Oakes--Renner relation, \texttt{cfl\_phase\_with\_gmor\_dual\_broken}, consumes both the contrapositive of the GMOR relation and the in-\CFL-phase magnitude positivity of the diquark condensate.
sentences.quote_normalized: a substantive cross-bridge to a companion paper on the gell-mann--oakes--renner relation, cfl\_phase\_with\_gmor\_dual\_broken, consumes both the contrapositive of the gmor relation and the in-\cfl-phase magnitude positivity of the diquark condensate.

sentences.quote: The conclusion is that the system is simultaneously chiral-broken and \CFL-broken --- an independent consistency check that the two breaking patterns occur in concert in the \CFL\ phase. Both hypotheses are load-bearing in the proof body.
sentences.quote_normalized: the conclusion is that the system is simultaneously chiral-broken and \cfl-broken --- an independent consistency check that the two breaking patterns occur in concert in the \cfl\ phase. both hypotheses are load-bearing in the proof body.

sentences.quote: The Lean module \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/CFLChiralLagrangian.lean} ships $13$ substantive theorems, zero \texttt{sorry} statements, and zero new axioms beyond the kernel's three.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean module lean/skefthawking/cflchirallagrangian.lean ships $13$ substantive theorems, zero sorry statements, and zero new axioms beyond the kernel's three.
sentences.agent_notes: IA DRIFT (repeat): paper says 13; live grep = 12. Memory says 12.

sentences.quote: The Python mirror in \texttt{src/cfl/} provides numerical evaluation of the cross-derivation identity: a $17$-case \texttt{pytest} suite includes a check that the \CFL\ emergent generator and the \QCD\ center generator agree to machine precision via independent code paths.
sentences.quote_normalized: the python mirror in src/cfl/ provides numerical evaluation of the cross-derivation identity: a $17$-case pytest suite includes a check that the \cfl\ emergent generator and the \qcd\ center generator agree to machine precision via independent code paths.
sentences.agent_notes: IA verified: 17 test methods in tests/test_cfl.py.

sentences.quote: The cross-derivation identification of the \CFL\ emergent $\Zthree$ generator with the \QCD\ center $\Zthree$ generator confirms the higher-form-symmetry framing of the quark--hadron continuity~\cite{HironoTanizaki2018,SchaeferWilczek1999} at the algebraic-generator level.
sentences.quote_normalized: the cross-derivation identification of the \cfl\ emergent $\zthree$ generator with the \qcd\ center $\zthree$ generator confirms the higher-form-symmetry framing of the quark--hadron continuity~ at the algebraic-generator level.
